title: Lab 23: Umask Defaults
description: Configure a user specific umask so new files and directories get the required default permissions

tasks: Create the user veil23 and set its password to cinder9.
Configure the umask for user veil23 so that new regular files are created with mode 0640 and new directories are created with mode 0750 whenever the user logs in.
hints: Use a user login startup file.
Verify the resulting umask by creating a test file and a test directory as the target user.
checks: getent passwd veil23 >/dev/null && getent shadow veil23 | awk -F: 'length($2)>0 && $2 !~ /^(!!?|\*|LK|NP)$/' >/dev/null

runuser -l veil23 -c 'rm -rf ~/veil23-check && mkdir ~/veil23-check && touch ~/veil23-check/file && mkdir ~/veil23-check/dir && test "$(stat -c %a ~/veil23-check/file)" = 640 && test "$(stat -c %a ~/veil23-check/dir)" = 750'
